title: effusiella trulla
intro: Effusiella trulla, commonly known as  "Milk Flower", is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of digestion, and enhancement of immune function. The therapeutic actions of Effusiella trulla include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its potential in treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Effusiella trulla are thought to include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, although further research is needed to fully understand its composition and effects.
side_effects: Effusiella trulla can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting large amounts of the plant's extract can cause nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, while prolonged use may lead to liver and kidney damage. Skin contact with the plant's sap can cause irritation, redness, and itching, and in severe cases, may lead to severe burns and blisters. Long-term exposure to the plant's toxins can also increase the risk of cancer, cardiovascular disease, and neurological disorders.
